title: Relative path in ENTRYPOINT handled differently than Docker
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

podman handles relative paths in ENTRYPOINT differently than docker does.

It also doesn't preserve empty WORKDIRs.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Given this Dockerfile:

```
FROM alpine:latest
WORKDIR /app
RUN cp /sbin/apk apk-copy
ENTRYPOINT ["./apk-copy", "--version"]
```

Docker runs the container as I expect:

```
$ docker build -t bug-demo .
[...]
$ docker run --rm -it bug-demo
apk-tools 2.10.4, compiled for x86_64.
```

But podman does not:

```
$ podman build -t bug-demo .
[...]
$ podman run --rm -it bug-demo
Error: executable file not fonud in $PATH: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

Two related but more hypothetical discrepancies I noticed while trying to understand what was going on here:

1. The WORKDIR command in Docker always creates the directory, whereas `podman build` only seems to omit the directory if no files are copied into it. So this Dockerfile also works with docker, but not podman (it complains it can't cd into the non-existent /app directory):

       FROM alpine:latest
       WORKDIR /app
       ENTRYPOINT ["echo", "hello"]

2. It seems like podman is instead treating `./sh` as just `sh` and looking it up in $PATH. For example, this Dockerfile runs /bin/sh with podman (seemingly incorrectly), but fails with Docker (seemingly correctly, because /app/sh doesn't exist):

       FROM alpine:latest
       WORKDIR /app
       RUN touch foo  # workaround above podman issue
       ENTRYPOINT ["./sh"]

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.6.2
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.1
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

comments.body: This would be a Buildah bug, and should be moved over there, once it is fixed there it would get revendored into Podman

The missing empty WORKDIR sounds like a buildah issue to me.

But I think the relative ENTRYPOINT is a podman issue, as `docker build` preserves the relative path in the container's ENTRYPOINT too:

```
$ docker image inspect bug-demo | grep -A4 WorkingDir
            "WorkingDir": "/app",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "./apk-copy",
                "--version"
            ],
--
            "WorkingDir": "/app",
            "Entrypoint": [
                "./apk-copy",
                "--version"
            ],
```

**Edit:** This is also what buildah produces:

```
# buildah inspect bug-demo | grep -A3 'Entrypoint": '
            "Entrypoint": [
                "./apk-copy",
                "--version"
            ],
--
            "Entrypoint": [
                "./apk-copy",
                "--version"
            ],
--
            "Entrypoint": [
                "./apk-copy",
                "--version"
            ],
```
You're right - the entrypoint is definitely a Podman issue. I'll take that one.
@mdempsky I cannot reproduce on Podman 1.6.2 or Master, root or rootless - it's running `apt` exactly as you would expect it would.

@mheon To be clear, you're saying that this Dockerfile builds and runs with podman for you?

```
FROM alpine:latest
WORKDIR /app
RUN cp /sbin/apk apk-copy
ENTRYPOINT ["./apk-copy", "--version"]
```

I'm still able to reproduce the issue locally on Fedora 31 using podman/buildah installed via dnf.

I'm trying to figure out the best way to make this standalone reproducible, but I'm not that familiar with containers and currently running into issues running podman inside of docker/podman. Going to look into just using qemu to boot the Fedora 31 live DVD.

Yes - I built the Dockerfile you provided and ran it via `podman run -t -i --rm $IMAGE` and it successfully printed the output of `apt version`. In the debug logs, I can see the relative path is being used in the container command.

@mheon What OCI runtime are you using? Could it be an issue with crun specifically? Looking at strace -f output, I see a process trying to stat "/usr/bin/./apk-copy", etc., and it looks like it's from /usr/bin/crun.

I'm on `runc` - this definitely sounds like it could be a `crun` issue

@giuseppe PTAL

Oh, looks like containers/crun#133.

Should I just file the empty WORKDIR issue against buildah and close this issue then?

Hm, actually the empty workdir problem goes away once I switch from `podman build` to `buildah bud`.

That is, given:

```
$ cat Dockerfile
FROM alpine:latest
WORKDIR /app
ENTRYPOINT ["echo", "hello"]
```

This works:

```
$ buildah bud -t demo -f Dockerfile
[...]
$ podman run --rm -it demo
hello
```
but this doesn't:

```
$ podman build -t demo -f Dockerfile
[...]
$ podman run --rm -it demo
Error: chdir: No such file or directory: OCI runtime command not found error
```

More curiously, `buildah version` reports 1.11.3, and [containers/libpod@v1.6.2's go.mod pins github.com/containers/buildah v1.11.3](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/v1.6.2/go.mod#L14).
This issue had no activity for 30 days. In the absence of activity or the "do-not-close" label, the issue will be automatically closed within 7 days.

I'm going to close this PR.  I've tested today and this is working in Podman upstream.  I do see the issue in Podman v1.6.2.  I believe it's fixed in Podman v1.6.3 but I unfortunately don't have easy access to an rpm at the moment to test with.

fedora 32
podman-2.0.5-1.fc32.x86_64
exactly the same problem still exists
https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/4429#issuecomment-549588997
